excluded_defs.name: clear
excluded_defs.reason: read to understand that it calls reset() which calls flushBuffer() which already resets buffering=true, so no changes needed to clear()

exploration_log.aha_moment: The existing flushBuffer() already sets buffering=true, meaning reset() already transitions back to buffering mode. The missing feature is a TIME-BASED automatic reset: record activatedAt in activate(), check elapsed time in handle(), and transition back to buffering when timeout expires.
exploration_log.hindsight: Reading the full class upfront was important. The reset() path already worked; only the automatic time-based path was missing.

alternatives: Add resetTimeout as a non-null required parameter
Add it as optional parameter with null default
key_decisions.decision: Optional parameter with null default (backward compatible)
key_decisions.reasoning: Existing code that doesn't use the feature should continue to work unchanged.

key_decisions.decision: Check in handle() before each record is processed
key_decisions.reasoning: The timeout should expire between records, not at activation time. handle() is called for each record so it's the right place to detect expiry.

search_sequence.result: Found activate() sets buffering=false when stopBuffering=true, handle() directs records based on buffering flag, reset()/flushBuffer() sets buffering=true. No time tracking anywhere.

search_sequence.action: Checked flushBuffer() and reset() to understand the existing reset path
search_sequence.reasoning: Task says to support reset when reset() is called — verified it already works
search_sequence.result: flushBuffer() sets buffering=true. reset() calls flushBuffer(). So reset() already transitions back.

queries.query_text: FingersCrossedHandler stays activated after flush, add resetTimeout to transition back to buffering mode

solve_notes: Added `resetTimeout` (nullable int, seconds) and `activatedAt` (nullable int) properties. In `activate()`, record `activatedAt = time()`. In `handle()`, before checking the buffering flag, check if timeout has elapsed and if so, reset `buffering = true`, clear buffer, clear `activatedAt`. In `flushBuffer()`, clear `activatedAt` so `reset()` also works correctly.
task_text:
Fix FingersCrossedHandler not resetting buffer after activation

After `FingersCrossedHandler` activates (threshold reached) and flushes
its buffer, subsequent records below the threshold continue to be
buffered but are never flushed because the handler remains in the
activated state. Fix the handler to transition back to buffering mode
after a configurable reset interval or when `reset()` is called.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: flushBuffer
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms that reset() already calls flushBuffer() which sets buffering=true. An agent needs to know this to avoid redundantly changing reset() and to ensure activatedAt is cleared in flushBuffer() for the reset() path.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: reset
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms reset() calls flushBuffer(), so the 'when reset() is called' requirement is already covered once flushBuffer() clears activatedAt

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers all edited locations. The thrash_preventing set adds flushBuffer() and reset() because an AI agent implementing this feature needs to verify that: (1) reset() already transitions back to buffering mode (via flushBuffer), so it doesn't need a separate implementation; (2) activatedAt must also be cleared in flushBuffer() so the reset() path doesn't leave stale activation timestamps.
